Description: India’s first high-speed railway, developed with Japanese ODA, engineering cooperation and Japanese Shinkansen technology. The project includes elevated viaducts, bridges, tunnels, stations, depots and high-speed rail systems connecting Mumbai and Ahmedabad.
Location: Maharashtra, Gujarat & Dadra and Nagar Haveli

Description: A large dedicated freight railway connecting northern India with western ports and industrial regions. Japan has provided major ODA financing and technical cooperation, including support for high-capacity electric freight infrastructure.
Location: Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan, Gujarat & Maharashtra

Project Name: Mumbai Trans Harbour Link – Atal Setu
Description: A 21.8-km sea-crossing connecting Mumbai with Navi Mumbai. Japan, through JICA ODA financing, played a major role in development of India’s longest sea bridge, featuring long marine viaducts and advanced bridge engineering.

Major bridge across the Brahmaputra connecting Assam and Meghalaya. Japanese ODA supports construction of this strategic road connection, which will significantly shorten travel distance between the two northeastern states.

Large orbital road project around Chennai connecting major highways, industrial areas and ports. JICA supports the project through Japanese ODA to strengthen freight movement and regional economic connectivity.

Project Name: North East Road Network Connectivity Improvement Project – Phase I
Description: Japan-supported road construction and upgrading program improving strategic highways through mountainous areas of Northeast India, including bridges, slope protection and resilient road engineering.

Project Name: North East Road Network Connectivity Improvement Project – Phase II
Description: Second major phase of Japan-supported road connectivity improvements, strengthening transport links between remote northeastern regions and major economic corridors.

Project Name: North East Road Network Connectivity Improvement Project – Phase III
Description: Continued development of strategic road infrastructure with Japanese assistance, including road widening, bridges, drainage, slope stabilization and improved regional connectivity.

Project Name: Bihar National Highway Improvement Project – Phase I
Description: Japanese ODA-supported highway improvement program involving road widening, pavement construction, bridges and related infrastructure to improve regional transportation in Bihar.

Project Name: Bihar National Highway Improvement Project – Phase II
Description: Further expansion and improvement of national highway infrastructure in Bihar with Japanese assistance, enhancing road capacity, safety and connectivity between important regional centers.

A massive India–Japan strategic infrastructure initiative covering industrial cities, freight connectivity, logistics, roads, utilities and smart urban infrastructure along the Delhi–Mumbai economic corridor.
Location: Delhi to Maharashtra across multiple states

Project Name: Dholera Special Investment Region / Industrial City
Description: Large greenfield industrial city under the DMIC framework, an India–Japan strategic initiative. Construction includes trunk roads, drainage, utilities, ICT infrastructure, water systems and large industrial zones.
India–Japan-supported economic corridor designed around integrated development of industrial zones, highways, ports, logistics and urban infrastructure between Chennai and Bengaluru.

Project Name: Tamil Nadu Investment Promotion / Industrial Infrastructure Program
Description: Japan-supported infrastructure and investment program improving roads, utilities, industrial parks and supporting infrastructure to strengthen manufacturing clusters, including areas hosting Japanese industries.

Major pumped-storage hydroelectric complex developed with Japanese ODA. Construction included upper and lower reservoirs, underground/large civil structures, waterways and a 900-MW pumped-storage generating facility.

Large pumped-storage hydropower development supported through Japanese ODA. It involves construction of reservoirs, dams, water-conveyance systems and an approximately 1,000-MW underground/associated power generation facility.
Pumped-storage hydroelectric project constructed with Japanese ODA assistance to meet peak electricity demand. Major works included reservoirs, waterways, tunnels and generating infrastructure.

Major power infrastructure development supported by Japanese ODA, involving construction of generating units and associated civil, mechanical and electrical infrastructure to expand electricity supply.

Port infrastructure modernization supported through Japanese ODA, aimed at improving cargo-handling capacity, port facilities and maritime logistics infrastructure at the strategically important Haldia port complex.

Project Name: Hooghly Dock and Port Engineers Modernization Project
Description: Japan-supported modernization of shipbuilding, dock and port-related industrial infrastructure, improving facilities and equipment for marine construction and vessel operations.

Major modernization of Indian steel-making infrastructure supported through Japanese ODA. Works involved industrial construction, plant facilities, equipment modernization and supporting civil infrastructure.

Large wastewater infrastructure program supported through Japanese ODA, involving sewage collection networks, treatment and disposal facilities designed to improve sanitation and coastal water quality in Mumbai.
Major Japan-supported environmental infrastructure program involving construction of sewerage networks, pumping stations and sewage treatment plants to reduce pollution entering the Yamuna River.

Project Name: Yamuna Action Plan Phase III – Delhi
Description: Major wastewater construction program supported through JICA, including rehabilitation and construction of sewage treatment facilities, sewer networks and related infrastructure in the Delhi region.

Project Name: Bengaluru Water Supply and Sewerage Project
Description: Large-scale urban water infrastructure development supported by Japanese ODA, involving water transmission pipelines, pumping facilities, reservoirs and sewerage systems for Bengaluru’s expanding population.

Project Name: Rajasthan Water Sector / Rural Water Supply Infrastructure Projects
Description: Japan-supported water infrastructure developments involving pipelines, treatment facilities, reservoirs and distribution systems designed to provide reliable water supply in water-scarce areas of Rajasthan.

Major underground metro construction project supported heavily through Japanese ODA. The corridor includes long underground twin tunnels, deep stations, depots and complex civil construction beneath densely developed Mumbai.

One of India’s most prominent examples of India–Japan infrastructure cooperation. Japanese ODA supported construction across several phases, with extensive elevated viaducts, tunnels, stations and rail systems.
Major urban rail infrastructure project supported by Japanese ODA. Construction includes underground tunnels, elevated viaducts, stations, depots and associated transit infrastructure across Chennai.

Project Name: High-Speed Rail Training Institute & Associated MAHSR Infrastructure, Vadodara
Description: Specialized infrastructure developed as part of the India–Japan high-speed rail cooperation program. It supports training of Indian engineers and personnel in Japanese Shinkansen-based railway construction, operation and maintenance.
